domain: Newar bahal courtyard community (Kathmandu Valley; courtyard-style Buddhist monastery + kinship compound; Guthi social-religious organization; decisions made in shared courtyard space with full process-level visibility of all members)
specific_mechanism: Shared-courtyard process-level context awareness — members observe each other's full operational state continuously, not just decision opinions; consensus is built on shared process transparency.
llm_application: BAHAL-PROCESS-LAYER: a shared, append-only process-context log for multi-agent LLM systems. Every agent writes its tool-calls, intermediate beliefs, recent failures, and active hypothesis to the SHARED log. Before any consensus-protocol decision, all agents READ the log (recent K entries) and condition their proposal on it. Conflict detection runs continuously on the log. Differs from voting/debate (opinion-level only) by maintaining a CONTINUOUS PROCESS-LEVEL telemetry log below opinions. Adjacent to Semantic Consensus Framework but distinct in providing CONTINUOUS PROCESS LOG (not snapshot-based conflict detection).
novelty_claim: Voting and debate protocols operate on OPINIONS. AgentsNet covers 5 distributed-computing primitives. Multi-Agent Debate trades arguments. Semantic Consensus Framework (2604.16339) has a Process Context Layer but for snapshot-based conflict detection. BAHAL-PROCESS-LAYER differs by (a) CONTINUOUS append-only process log, (b) ALL agents read before any proposal, (c) PROCESS-level shared context (not opinion-level), (d) conflict detection ON the live log.
